description_long: This object is a tall coconut palm tree with a slender, textured trunk that extends vertically and culminates in a crown of green, feathery fronds. The trunk is brown and appears to have a rough, fibrous surface with subtle vertical ridges, typical of coconut palms. At the top, the fronds radiate outward in a circular pattern, with some leaves drooping and others splayed horizontally, creating a lush canopy. The leaves are long, narrow, and segmented, with a vibrant green color that fades slightly at the tips. No coconuts are visibly attached to the fronds or trunk in any of the views. The overall appearance is consistent with a mature coconut palm commonly found in tropical urban parks or along city streets.
description: A tall, slender coconut palm tree with a rough brown trunk and a crown of green, feathery fronds. No coconuts are visible on the tree.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full height of the palm with a straight trunk and a symmetrical spread of fronds at the top.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the trunk's slenderness and the fronds' outward extension, with some leaves more visible on the right.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the trunk's texture and a slightly different arrangement of the fronds, with more leaves visible on the left.
description_view_3: Another side view, showing the trunk's verticality and a few fronds extending more prominently to the left.
attribute_husk_pattern: The trunk displays a vertically ridged, fibrous texture with a rough, irregular pattern. The fibers run parallel along the length of the trunk, creating a dense, linear arrangement with some subtle variations in thickness and spacing.
attribute_proximity_to_palm_trees: The object itself is a palm tree; no other palm trees are visible nearby.
